chapter: Chapter: Civilising the Native, Educating the Nation

questions.question: What were the traditional schools in India called before British rule?
questions.explanation: Pathshalas and gurukuls were traditional institutions of learning in India.

questions.question: Which group supported Indian languages and classical texts in education?
questions.explanation: Orientalists believed that Indian languages and classical knowledge should be promoted.

questions.question: Which group believed English should be the medium of instruction in India?
questions.explanation: Anglicists believed Western knowledge should be taught through English.

questions.question: Who wrote the famous Minute on Indian Education in 1835?
questions.explanation: Thomas Babington Macaulay wrote the Minute on Indian Education in 1835.

questions.question: What was the main idea of Macaulay’s Minute?

options.C: Promote English education and Western knowledge

questions.explanation: Macaulay believed English education should spread Western knowledge.

questions.question: Which document laid the foundation for the modern education system in India?
questions.explanation: Wood’s Despatch of 1854 recommended universities and educational reforms.

questions.question: Which universities were established in India in 1857?
questions.explanation: The first universities were established in Calcutta, Bombay, and Madras.

questions.question: Which Indian reformer strongly supported modern education?
questions.explanation: Raja Rammohan Roy supported modern scientific education.

questions.question: What was one objective of British education policies?
questions.explanation: British education policies aimed to spread Western knowledge.

questions.question: What was the result of English education in India?
questions.explanation: English education helped create a new educated middle class.

questions.question: Which ideas spread through Western education in India?
questions.explanation: Western education introduced ideas such as democracy and equality.

questions.question: Why did traditional education decline under British rule?
questions.explanation: British policies favored English education over traditional institutions.

questions.question: Which subject was promoted through Western education?
questions.explanation: Western education introduced subjects like science and mathematics.

questions.question: Which class emerged due to modern education in India?
questions.explanation: Modern education led to the emergence of an educated middle class.

questions.question: What role did education play in social reforms?
questions.explanation: Education helped people become aware of social problems and promote reforms.

questions.question: Which idea helped Indians question colonial rule?
questions.explanation: Ideas of democracy and equality inspired Indians to challenge colonial rule.

questions.question: What language became important in higher education during British rule?
questions.explanation: English became the main language of higher education.

questions.question: Which reform recommended teacher training institutions?
questions.explanation: Wood’s Despatch recommended teacher training institutions.

questions.question: Which movement benefited from modern education in India?
questions.explanation: Educated Indians became leaders of the freedom movement.

questions.question: Which system influenced modern Indian education?
questions.explanation: Colonial education introduced universities and modern subjects.

questions.question: Which city had one of the first universities in India?
questions.explanation: The University of Calcutta was established in 1857.

questions.question: Which concept spread through modern education?
questions.explanation: Education spread ideas of equality and rights.

questions.question: Why did some Indians criticize colonial education?
questions.explanation: Some Indians felt colonial education neglected Indian culture and traditions.

questions.question: What was a long-term effect of colonial education?
questions.explanation: Colonial education introduced universities and examination systems.

questions.question: Which factor helped spread political awareness in India?
questions.explanation: Western education introduced political ideas that increased awareness.
